purpose: The architectural RISK register — structural properties that make the system fragile, independent of any specific bug. A risk is a shape, not an incident.
scope_note: *** This index records RISK, not REMEDIATION. The fixes belong to ../REMEDIATION_OPTIONS.md and ../IMPLEMENTATION_SEQUENCE.md (Cycle 4). Cycle 5 does not design fixes. ***

risks.risk: THE SUBMISSION LIFECYCLE HAS NO OWNER
risks.structural_statement: `publish` CREATES posts in a state it will never re-drive. `reconcile` is that state's SOLE reader and cannot always terminate it. No component owns the interval between 'we sent it' and 'we know what happened'.
risks.why_it_is_a_RISK_not_just_a_BUG: Even after RC-1/RC-2 are fixed, NOTHING in the architecture assigns that ownership. The next feature that writes `submitting` re-opens the hole.
evidence: reconcile.py:739/746/757/767 (three independent exclusions from the terminal)
risks.worst_credible_consequence: A post the operator believes is publishing sits in `submitting` indefinitely. Never published, never failed, never surfaced, never counted. SILENT NON-DELIVERY.
risks.reachable_by: AN ORDINARY OPERATOR WORKFLOW — a platform-rejected post, sent back to fix the caption, re-approved.

risks.risk: TWO LOCK DOMAINS THAT DO NOT EXCLUDE EACH OTHER
risks.structural_statement: The ledger (SQLite BEGIN IMMEDIATE) and the control files (fcntl.flock) are mutually invisible. And `restore_snapshot` takes a flock on a THIRD file that participates in NO domain, then os.replace's the database.
risks.the_deeper_fact: *** os.replace of a SQLite file is FUNDAMENTALLY INCOMPATIBLE with SQLite's inode-based locking. The write lock is held on the INODE via an open fd. NO lock acquired before an os.replace can protect it. *** Taking the right lock narrows the window; it does not close it.
risks.the_inversion: The deferred-unlink design ('a rolled-back txn never deletes a file it did not drop') is CORRECT against a rollback and INVERTS against a restore race: the txn COMMITS into an orphan, so it is never rolled back, so the unlinks PROCEED. *** The ledger comes back claiming the media exists. The .mp4 is gone. ***
risks.worst_credible_consequence: SILENT, UNRECOVERABLE LOSS OF MEDIA, reached by following the DOCUMENTED wipe-rollback procedure while the daemon is running.

risks.risk: THE VERIFICATION LAYER CAN ENCODE THE DEFECT
risks.structural_statement: *** A GREEN CI TEST ASSERTS THE DATA-LOSS OUTCOME AND CALLS IT CORRECT. *** tests/test_ledger_sqlite_store.py:161-186 stages exactly the restore race. Its comment names a mechanism that does not exist ('restorer blocked on flock held by writer' — the restorer is NEVER blocked; measured at 0.001s). Its assertion PASSES *BECAUSE* the writer's committed row was destroyed.
risks.why_this_is_the_most_dangerous_entry_in_: It is a REGRESSION LOCK ON THE BUG. Any correct fix turns it RED — and a maintainer seeing a green test go red will read THE FIX as the regression.
risks.the_generalization: The codebase's recurring shape is 'the doc names a mechanism that does not exist, while the property survives via a different one.' This is that shape IN THE TEST LAYER. *** For every invariant an audit claims is protected by a test, read the test's ASSERTION, not its NAME. ***

risks.risk: IMPORT-TIME ACYCLICITY IS PURCHASED, NOT DESIGNED
risks.structural_statement: *** 107 of 323 in-function imports point to an EQUAL-OR-HIGHER LEVEL: 56 STRICTLY UPWARD (true layering inversions) + 51 LATERAL. *** The SCC-CONDENSED compile-time graph is an 11-level DAG ONLY because those imports are deferred to call time. In the STATIC POTENTIAL-DEPENDENCY graph (compile UNION lazy) the tree contains a 45-MODULE SCC and all 19 subsystems collapse into one — an UPPER BOUND on coupling, not a measurement of it.
risks.the_sharpest_instance: *** `config` — LEVEL 0, fan-in 82, THE most-depended-on module in the system — lazily reaches UP to `accounts` (level 2) and `meta_graph` (level 3). The base of the stack calls the middle of it. ***
risks.why_it_is_a_RISK: Module initialization order is LOAD-BEARING and NOTHING ENFORCES IT. Hoisting any one of the 56 STRICTLY-UPWARD lazy imports to module level — a change that looks like a cleanup — breaks the process at start. There is no test, no lint rule, and no layer declaration that would catch it.
risks.supersedes: COUP-09 recorded NINE such imports and framed them as a deliberate, bounded cycle-breaking device. The nine are a SUBSET of the 107 (56 strictly upward). *** HOW MANY OF THE REST ARE DELIBERATE IS UNKNOWN (UNK-C5-2) — the v1 index asserted 'most were not deliberate', which is NOT SUPPORTED by any evidence and is RETRACTED. ***
risks.scope_correction: *** The compile-time graph is NOT a DAG — it has one non-trivial SCC (personas x3). Its CONDENSATION is a DAG by construction. And the DOMAIN CORE IS ACYCLIC AT MODULE LEVEL: 124 of 127 modules are singleton SCCs. The v1 index conflated 'condensed DAG' with 'DAG' and reported 106 instead of 107. ***

risks.risk: A FREE-TEXT FIELD IS A MACHINE CONTROL CHANNEL
risks.structural_statement: Post.error_reason has ~14 writers and FOUR machine semantics: a retry COUNTER, a terminal MARKER, a quarantine SENTINEL, and a do-not-look-again LATCH.
risks.the_pathology: *** The breadcrumb that tells the operator a post is stuck IS the same mechanism that stops the system from ever looking at it again (reconcile.py:767: `if post.error_reason: continue`). A status field and a suppression flag CANNOT be the same string. ***
risks.compounding: bulk_send_to_review clears scheduled_time, public_url, metrics and published_at — but NOT error_reason. So a reverted post carries a STALE reason from its previous life, which trips the latch ON THE FIRST PASS. The post is silent from pass one and its displayed reason actively LIES about its state.

risks.risk: A PRODUCER AND ITS SOLE CONSUMER ARE GATED ON DIFFERENT PREDICATES
risks.structural_statement: _reconcile_safe runs only `if cfg.is_live_backend` (pipeline.py:318). _publish_safe has NO such gate (pipeline.py:334). *** The SOLE READER of `submitting` can be switched OFF while the WRITER keeps producing it. ***
risks.generalized: A state with a producer and no consumer is a LEAK BY CONSTRUCTION. Nothing in the architecture pairs them.

risks.risk: GATE STRENGTH IS INVERSELY CORRELATED WITH BLAST RADIUS
risks.structural_statement: *** The ONE IRREVERSIBLE learning actuator (adjust.retire -> MomentState.retired, which reconcile_moments REFUSES to un-retire) is gated ONLY on cfg.is_live_backend and fires at n=3 analyzed posts. Every REVERSIBLE bias actuator waits for learning_validated AND p4_unlocked (>=8 posts x >=2 values). ***
risks.why_it_is_a_RISK_not_a_DEFECT: The guards that DO exist are real and considered (bottom-20% AND lift<20.0 AND not-a-winner AND not-lift_degraded) — which is EVIDENCE FOR INTENT. Whether this is an intentionally aggressive policy or an oversight is NOT RECOVERABLE FROM THE CODE.
risks.status: *** BLOCKED ON PD-3. The brief forbids inferring product intent. Filed, not fixed. ***

risks.risk: VALIDATORS RUN AT CONSTRUCTION ONLY; EVERY MUTATION API BYPASSES THEM
risks.structural_statement: model_copy(update=) bypasses EVERY validator on EVERY model. setattr bypasses all EXCEPT on Moment. The failure mode is NOT a rejected write — it is a LOAD-TIME POISON PILL: the bad row SAVES CLEANLY and BRICKS THE NEXT Ledger.load, taking down the daemon and every Studio page at once.
risks.current_reachability: *** ZERO. No code path produces the poison row. *** AST-verified across three cycles.
risks.why_it_stays_in_the_register: The R1 invariant survives via FOUR INDEPENDENT MANUAL GUARDS. A FIFTH DOOR added without one is a single-commit path to a bricked ledger, and nothing would catch it in review.

risks.risk: AN UNMODELLED SECOND WRITER: THE OPERATOR
risks.structural_statement: accounts.json, tuning.json and .env are DOCUMENTED as hand-editable. The code's write boundaries (set_backend etc.) are sound; the LOAD boundaries are not. *** Every load-boundary validation gap is therefore REACHABLE BY DESIGN, not by accident. ***
risks.the_instance: RC-3 — a hand-edited 'Postiz' (capital P) or 'postiz ' (trailing space) resolves to a DryRunPoster ON A LIVE SYSTEM, silently.

risks.risk: `alive` CONFLATES 'THE PROCESS IS RUNNING' WITH 'THE WORK IS SUCCEEDING'
risks.structural_statement: SIX distinct liveness facts are collapsed into ONE word: process alive / loop ticking / pass started / *pass SUCCEEDED* / *state ADVANCED* / *publishing healthy*. daemon status reports the first two and names the result `alive`, which the operator reads as the fourth.
risks.the_data_already_exists: heartbeat_age_s IS in the returned dict, and the heartbeat fires ONLY when a pass succeeds — *** it already IS the success signal. It is simply not READ as one. The VERDICT discards it. ***
risks.worst_consequence: the operator asks the system's own liveness question and is told `alive` while nothing publishes.

risks.structural_statement: compress.maybe_shrink_for_cap mkdtemps into 04_agent_io. It is the ONLY mkdtemp in src/fanops (AST) and there is NO rmtree anywhere in the tree that touches it. One dir + one re-encoded mp4 per oversize upload, FOREVER, with NO log, NO metric, NO doctor check and NO wipe path.

risks.risk: THE 'THIS CAN'T HAPPEN' COMMENT IS A RELIABLE DEFECT MARKER
risks.structural_statement: *** FOUR FOR FOUR. *** Every load-bearing assumption written as a statement of fact sits at the exact site of a live bug:

instances.reality: it does not resolve when the platform deleted the post. C3-F1.

instances.comment: 'no live account routes to an unknown backend'
instances.reality: a hand-edit — the DOCUMENTED operator channel — routes exactly there. F-A.

instances.comment: 'restorer blocked on flock held by writer'
instances.reality: the restorer is NEVER blocked. Measured: 0.001s. RC-5.

instances.comment: deferred unlink is 'correct: a rolled-back txn never deletes a file it did not drop'
instances.reality: TRUE against a rollback; the txn COMMITS into an orphan in a restore race. C4-F2a.

risks.actionable_method_note: *** grep the tree for comments containing 'never', 'cannot', 'WILL', or 'no ... routes to'. Each is a HYPOTHESIS THE AUTHOR DID NOT TEST. ***

risks.structural_statement: 149 Studio routes, 108 mutating, 0 authenticated, 0 CSRF-protected — including POST /golive/live (flip to live publishing), POST /schedule/publish-due (publish the whole due bucket) and POST /live-library/wipe/confirm (destructive wipe).
risks.status: *** A RECORDED, ACCEPTED DECISION (studio/CLAUDE.md: 'no auth by design ... declined as out-of-scope for localhost'). Recorded here as GROUND TRUTH, not as a recommendation. ***
risks.the_only_control: the network interface (app.run(host=...), default 127.0.0.1:8787). *** Any change to --host removes it. ***

risks.risk: A REGISTRY THAT MUST BE EDITED IN N PLACES WILL EVENTUALLY BE EDITED IN N-1
risks.structural_statement: The gate-kind registry is TRIPLICATED and UNLINKED: the WRITE site, responder._SCHEMA (the ANSWER registry), and gate_keys.gate_source_id (the OWNERSHIP resolver). *** NOTHING — no test, no type, no assertion — ties the three together. ***
risks.the_proof_it_is_live: intro_match is registered in ONE of the three, and is therefore PERMANENTLY UNANSWERABLE. It is the standing demonstration of this risk, shipped.
risks.sibling_instance: _VALID_BACKENDS is defined TWICE (config.py:72 and settings.py:18) — one guards the WRITE boundary, one the READ boundary. If they drift, the two boundaries disagree about what a legal backend is. That is exactly the gap RC-3 exploits.

risks.risk: PROCESS-LOCAL STATE IN A SYSTEM THAT MAY GO MULTI-PROCESS
risks.structural_statement: run._publish_throttle_last is a MODULE-LEVEL DICT. *** If fanops ever runs as N concurrent publisher processes, the Postiz per-minute throttle becomes silently per-process and N processes publish N x the rate limit. *** Self-declared in post/CLAUDE.md; single-process by design today.

risk_ranking_by_what_it_actually_costs: AR-02 + AR-03 (media loss + the test that locks it in) — CRITICAL, and AR-03 makes AR-02 harder to fix than to find
AR-01 + AR-06 (the submission lifecycle has no owner, and its sole reader can be switched off) — HIGH
AR-09 (the operator is an unmodelled writer) — HIGH, and it is the door AR-01's worst variant comes through
AR-04 (import acyclicity is purchased, not designed) — MEDIUM, but it is the risk that makes every OTHER refactor dangerous
AR-08, AR-11, AR-15 — LOW today; AR-08 is CRITICAL if a fifth door is ever added
AR-13 — ACCEPTED, recorded, not a finding
